{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query asks which approach cannot be used for the removal of a vertebral disc. From the documents, we see that laminotomy, laminectomy, and hemilaminectomy are all mentioned as procedures that can be used to remove a herniated disc. Laminoplasty, however, is described as a procedure that involves opening the lamina to provide more space for the spinal cord, rather than directly removing the disc material. Therefore, laminoplasty is the approach that cannot be used for the removal of a vertebral disc.", "answer_choice": "C"}
